question:
Why is it generally considered poor social etiquette to check your phone repeatedly during a one-on-one conversation?
A) Phones emit radiation that harms the other person.
B) It signals that whatever is on your screen takes priority over the person in front of you, making them feel devalued.
C) It is only rude if the other person is your employer.
D) Checking your phone legally voids any agreement made during the conversation.
